Regardless of the result, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to attack rolls against the creature and to your AC and saving throws against attacks and effects from the creature for 1 minute. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-effects.Item.P3lpqXMd5FbUF57c]{Effect: Sash of Books (Bonus)}
entries.pf2e.specificrule.equipment.scaldinggaun: A creature you grab or restrain while wearing the gauntlets must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:26|options:damaging-effect,inflicts:sickened|showDC:all] save or take @Damage[2d6[persistent,fire]] damage and be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} from the pain; it's temporarily immune to being sickened by scalding gauntlets for 1 hour.

A creature you grab or restrain while wearing the gauntlets must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:28|options:damaging-effect,inflicts:sickened|showDC:all] save or take @Damage[2d6[persistent,fire]] damage and be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} from the pain; it's temporarily immune to being sickened by scalding gauntlets for 1 hour.

A creature you grab or restrain while wearing the gauntlets must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:32|options:damaging-effect,inflicts:sickened|showDC:all] save or take @Damage[3d6[persistent,fire]] damage and be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} from the pain; it's temporarily immune to being sickened by scalding gauntlets for 1 hour.

A creature you grab or restrain while wearing the gauntlets must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:36|options:damaging-effect,inflicts:sickened|showDC:all] save or take @Damage[4d6[persistent,fire]] damage and be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} from the pain; it's temporarily immune to being sickened by scalding gauntlets for 1 hour.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.equipment.scapularofsh: Activate Scapular of Shields to gain critical immunity.
Activate Scapular of Shields to gain resistance to all damage.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.equipment.serpentdagge: When you critically succeed at an attack roll with the serpent dagger, the target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} unless it succeeds at a @Check[fortitude|dc:19] save.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.equipment.shawlofseaso: Shawl of Seasons — Change of Seasons
Shawl of Seasons — Benefit matches season
entries.pf2e.specificrule.equipment.sparkler.cri: On a critical hit, you cause the target to become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.equipment.tacticianshe: On a successful @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.KAVf7AmRnbCAHrkT]{Reactive Strike}, add one charge: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-effects.Item.nLwtvyLZZTZgFmKq]{Effect: Tactician's Helm Charges}
entries.pf2e.specificrule.equipment.talentedtaps: When you succeed or critically succeed at a Reflex save to avoid a damaging effect, you can Stride half your Speed as a reaction.
Toothy Knife — Adjust for assisted recovery
On a critical hit with the horns, the target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:30] or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} on a critical failure).
Can damage only creatures with the holy trait.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.equipment.wemmuthtrink: Creatures who fail or critically fail their Reflex saves also take @Damage[(@item.rank)[piercing]].
entries.pf2e.specificrule.eternallegend.deathfro: The target dealt damage by your Strike must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|against:class] or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.eternallegend.dedicati: Eternal Legend — Apply adjustment against condition
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.compliantgold: Compliant Gold — Weapon already had reach.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.destinedvicto: Whenever an enemy successfully Strikes you with a melee weapon, you gain a +2 status bonus to your AC against the next attack from that enemy before the start of your next turn.
Whenever the enemy misses you with a Strike, you heal @Damage[(1d8+5)[healing]] Hit Points.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.divinespark.s: To use this action, your divine spark must be empowering the chosen ikon and you must have the ikon ready to use.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.epithet.borno: When you critically succeed on a Strike and the target of the critical hit is standing on a surface of earth or stone, the target is driven down and mired in the ground. The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} and must succeed at an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escape} attempt against your class DC to end the immobilization. The creature doesn't become stuck if it is incorporeal, is liquid (like a water elemental or some oozes), has a burrow speed, or could otherwise escape without effort.

When you Spark Transcendence while standing on a surface of earth or stone, you can choose to shatter the surface in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10], making it difficult terrain. You are not affected by this difficult terrain.
Born of the Bones of the Earth
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.epithet.dance: When you critically succeed on a Strike, you can Step as a free action.

When you Spark Transcendence, you gain temporary Hit Points equal to half your level as you are reinvigorated by the changing of the seasons; these temporary Hit Points last until the start of your next turn.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.epithet.heale: After you Spark Transcendence, choose any number of willing or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} creatures in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30]. Each of these creatures that's dying loses the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yZRUzMqrMmfLu0V1]{Dying} condition and remains unconscious at 0 Hit Points, and each one that isn't dying gains 10 temporary Hit Points that last until the start of your next turn. This is a healing vitality effect.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.epithet.label: Epithet
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.epithet.ofver: When you critically succeed on a Strike, haunting melodies play around the target, making them @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} unless they succeed on a @Check[will|against:exemplar] save against your class DC.

When you Spark Transcendence, your divine spark releases a sublime song that harmonizes with your allies. Until the start of your next turn, any of your allies that starts their turn within 30 feet of you can hum to Sustain one of their effects that can be sustained. This is a free action triggered by the ally's turn beginning. Your song then ends, and that ally can't benefit from this ability again for 10 minutes. This is an auditory and mental effect.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.epithet.peerl: After you Spark Transcendence, your impeccable battle form strikes fear. An enemy of your choice within 30 feet must succeed at a @Check[will|against:exemplar|traits:emotion,fear,mental,visual] save against your class DC or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}. That creature is then temporarily immune to this effect for 10 minutes. This is an emotion, fear, mental, and visual effect.
Peerless Under Heaven — Gain Grievous Rune critical specialization
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.epithet.plund: When you critically succeed at a Strike, the taste of honey fills your mouth as you plunder part of your foe's vitality for yourself, gaining temporary Hit Points equal to half the creature's level that last for 1 minute.

When you Spark Transcendence, a mass of buzzing insects fill the air around you, granting you @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealment} until the beginning of your next turn.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.epithet.restl: When you critically succeed on a Strike, water blasts the target and those nearby. This deals bludgeoning splash damage equal to the number of weapon damage dice to the target and all creatures within 10 feet of it. This effect has the water trait.

When you Spark Transcendence, you can Step, your body carried along by a surging tide. If your transcendence affected an enemy, you can instead move that enemy 5 feet in a direction of your choice unless it succeeds at a @Check[fortitude|against:exemplar] save against your class DC. If you move an enemy who started out adjacent to you, you can Step into the space it vacated.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.epithet.teach: After you Spark Transcendence, you can attempt to Recall Knowledge about one enemy you can see as a free action and instantly transmit any knowledge you gain as a result to every ally within 60 feet. The first piece of knowledge you gain when Recalling Knowledge in this way is always the enemy's immunities. If the enemy has no immunities, you learn its greatest resistance; if it also possesses no resistances, you learn its greatest weakness; if it possesses none of the above, you learn its lowest saving throw.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.epithet.thebr: Your body carries you forward, allowing you to Stride up to half your Speed in a straight line toward one enemy of your choice as a free action. Once you have used this ability on a given enemy, you can't use it against that enemy again for 10 minutes.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.epithet.thecu: After you Spark Transcendence, you can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.GkmbTGfg8KcgynOA]{Create a Diversion} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.QNAVeNKtHA0EUw4X]{Feint} as a free action.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.epithet.thede: When you Spark Transcendence, you can Interact as a free action to reload or draw a weapon ikon, either directly before or directly after your transcendence action. The weapon ikon must be a ranged weapon with the reload trait or a one-handed melee weapon with the thrown trait.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.epithet.thela: After you Spark Transcendence, you can exude an air of authority until the start of your next turn. If any enemy fails at an attack roll against you during this time, you can attempt an Intimidation check to [[/act demoralize]] that enemy as a free action as you rebuke it for its foolish attempt to stand against your authority.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.epithet.themo: After you Spark Transcendence, your act resonates with bittersweet poignancy, making one enemy of your choice within 30 feet who witnessed the act @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} as tears or memories dance in their eyes. This is an emotion and mental effect. The enemy remains dazzled until the start of your next turn, and then they are temporarily immune for 10 minutes.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.epithet.thepr: After you Spark Transcendence, you can boast to one enemy within 30 feet to draw its attention; this effect has the auditory, emotion, mental, and linguistic traits. Until the start of your next turn, the target takes a –1 status penalty to attack rolls, damage rolls, and skill checks against creatures other than you, and it gains a +1 status bonus to these rolls when targeting you.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.epithet.thera: After you Spark Transcendence, you inspire an ally within 30 feet to push on, restoring Hit Points equal to @Damage[(2*(1+@actor.level))[healing]]{2 + double your level}; this is a mental and emotion effect. The ally is then temporarily immune for 10 minutes.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.epithet.thief: After you Spark Transcendence, as a free action you can attempt a check to [[/act palm-an-object]] or to [[/act steal]] an item from an enemy within 30 feet. The relocated objects simply disappear and reappear in your hand.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.epithet.tresp: When you critically succeed at a Strike, you pull your target closer to death from your stolen connection. The target must attempt a @Check[will|against:exemplar|traits:curse] saving against your class DC. On a failure, they become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 minute. This has the curse trait.

When you Spark Transcendence, your divine spark draws part of death closer to you. Until the start of your next turn, any living enemy that starts it turns adjacent to you or enters a space adjacent to you takes @Damage[1d6[void]] damage.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.epithet.whose: When you critically succeed on a Strike, the target must make a @Check[fortitude|against:exemplar|traits:sonic] saving throw against your class DC. On a failure, they are knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.9PR9y0bi4JPKnHPR]{Deafened} for 1 minute. This is a sonic effect.

When you Spark Transcendence, you can choose to become electrically charged until the start of your next turn. Any creature that touches you or damages you with an unarmed attack or non-reach melee weapon while you're charged takes @Damage[1d6[electricity]] damage as lightning courses back to them. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.nP3k4N52NzrR3KKf]{Effect: Whose Cry is Thunder}
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.eternityincin: Whenever you score a critical hit with the imbued ikon, the target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 1} or increases its doomed condition by 1. If the target's maximum dying value is reduced to 0 by this ability, it immediately dies in a flash of spiritual fire that reduces its body to ash.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.hurlatthehori: The weapon's thrown distance is increased by 10 feet.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.ikon.barrowse: Barrows Edge — Target is below half HP
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.ikon.physical: Use an existing item in my inventory.
What item will be your ikon?

entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.ikon.shadowsh: You can spend 1 minute to load this weapon into the sheath.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.ikon.skinhard: If a creature attacking you using a weapon dealing the same damage type as your resistance misses you or hits you but deals no damage due to your resistance, the weapon clangs wildly off your skin. This painful reverberation makes the attacking enemy off-guard and gives it a –2 circumstance penalty to attacks with that weapon until the start of the enemy's next turn. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.sEWwGSuXxcTl020D]{Effect: Crash Against Me (Clang)}
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.infiniteblade: Whenever you successfully Strike an enemy with your weapon ikon, up to two enemies adjacent to the target take @Damage[@item.system.damage.dice@item.system.damage.die[spirit]] damage as they are struck by duplicated missiles.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.matedbirdsinp: Rejoin in Flight — Additional weapon damage die
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.sevencoloredc: When you successfully Strike an enemy, you can choose to teleport to an unoccupied location within 10 feet as a free action.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.shiftimmanenc: You can also Shift Immanence as a free action triggered when you roll initiative.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplar.sunwrecker.no: On a successful critical hit with the weapon, the weapon casts 2nd-rank darkness centered on the target of the attack.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplaryfinisher.batt: You can Step as a free action immediately after the finisher.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplaryfinisher.brag: If the foe was temporarily immune to your @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.2u915NdUyQan6uKF]{Demoralize}, their temporary immunity ends.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplaryfinisher.fenc: The foe is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until your next turn.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplaryfinisher.name: Exemplary Finisher
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplaryfinisher.rasc: The foe takes a -10 circumstance penalty to its speed until the start of your next turn. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.fgz7lwg1xHsVW4dX]{Effect: Exemplary Finisher (Rascal)}
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exemplaryfinisher.wit: The foe takes a -2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls against you until the start of your next turn. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.cu10OjQUdY0a9gpj]{Effect: Exemplary Finisher (Wit)}
entries.pf2e.specificrule.exorcistdedication.spi: Your Spirit Dwelling contains a spirit
Select the cost of your Spirit's Mercy
Select the level of your Spirit Remnant
entries.pf2e.specificrule.familiarsage.lightning: Did the Familiar Sage cast a spell with the air or electricity trait this turn?
entries.pf2e.specificrule.fanesfourberie.prompt: Treat Cards as darts or daggers?
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.bespellstrikes.or: You siphon spell energy into one weapon you're wielding, or into one of your unarmed attacks, such as a fist. Until the end of your turn, the weapon or unarmed attack deals an extra 1d6 force damage and gains the divine trait if it didn't have it already. If the spell dealt a different type of damage, the Strike deals this type of damage instead (or one type of your choice if the spell could deal multiple types of damage).
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.bespellstrikes.re: Your most recent action was to cast a non-cantrip spell
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.bespellstrikes.so: You siphon spell energy into one weapon you're wielding or into one of your unarmed attacks, such as a fist. Until the end of your turn, the weapon or unarmed attack deals an extra 1d6 force damage and gains the trait of your bloodline's magical tradition if it didn't have it already. If the spell dealt a different type of damage, the Strike deals this type of damage instead (or one type of your choice if the spell could deal multiple types of damage).
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.bespellstrikes.wi: You siphon spell energy into one weapon you're wielding, or into one of your unarmed attacks, such as a fist. Until the end of your turn, the weapon or unarmed attack deals an extra 1d6 force damage and gains the arcane trait if it didn't have it already. If the spell dealt a different type of damage, the Strike deals this type of damage instead (or one type of your choice if the spell could deal multiple types of damage).
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.burnit.label: Burn It!
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.charmingliar.note: When you get a critical success using the Lie action, the target's attitude toward you improves by one step, as though you'd succeeded at using Diplomacy to Make an Impression.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.disturbingknowled: You get overly caught up in your own words and become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.
The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 round and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.experiencedprofes: If you're an expert in Lore, you gain twice as much income from a failed check to Earn Income, unless it was originally a critical failure.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.fastrecovery.note: If you roll a success on a Fortitude save against an ongoing poison or disease, reduce its stage by 2, or by 1 if its virulent. If you roll a critical success, reduce its stage by 3, or by 2 if its virulent.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.forager.note: While using Survival to Subsist, if you roll any result worse than a success, you get a success. You can also provide for multiple creatures.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.greenwatcher.note: Whenever you critically succeed on a Strike against a fey creature, the target is immediately subject to the effects of a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.HRb2doyaLtaoCfi3]{Faerie Fire} spell. For the purposes of counteracting this effect, it's an innate 2nd-rank primal spell.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.incrediblefamilia: Your connection to your apparition and your mastery of spiritual magic reach a new threshold that allows you to channel even more power into the physical form you allow it to take on as your familiar. You can select a base of six familiar or master abilities each day, instead of four.

By taking the best parts of each magical tradition, you've found a way to grant your familiar even more abilities than other familiars. You can select a base of six familiar or master abilities each day, instead of four.
Your familiar is imbued with even more magic than other familiars. You can select a base of six familiar or master abilities each day, instead of four.
Add the bonus familiar abilities you gain for being a witch to this amount.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.intensifiedelemen: Intensified Element Stance — Damage vs. Constructs
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.knowitall.bard: When you succeed at a check to Recall Knowledge, you gain additional information or context. When you critically succeed at a check to Recall Knowledge, you get additional information or context or can ask an additional follow-up question (the GM chooses which).
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.knowitall.thaumat: Having heard every rumor or story in the book, you know that if you've heard of something in the first place, you've probably heard about it at length. When you succeed at a check to Recall Knowledge, you gain additional information or context about the given topic. When you critically succeed at a check to Recall Knowledge, at the GM's discretion, you might gain even more additional information or context than normal.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.legendarycodebrea: If you Decipher Writing at the normal rate, and you roll a success, you get a critical success; if you critically succeed while spending the normal amount of time, you gain a nearly word-for-word understanding of the document.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.lightningswap.exe: You have practiced quickly switching between combat styles and the equipment needed for them, especially if you wield more than one weapon ikon. You Interact to stow any number of items from your hands, then draw up to two weapons or a shield and a weapon.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.lightningswap.fig: You have practiced quickly switching between combat styles and the equipment needed for them. You Interact to stow any number of items from your hands, then draw up to two weapons or a shield and a weapon.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.peerbeyond.note: You can roll a Spirit Lore or Haunt Lore check for initiative if you know that an incorporeal undead or a haunt is present.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.quickclimb.note: You move 5 more feet on a success and 10 more feet on a critical success, up to your Speed.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.quickswim.note: You Swim 5 feet farther on a success and 10 feet farther on a critical success, to a maximum of your Speed.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.riskysurgery.note: When you Treat Wounds, you can deal @Damage[1d8[slashing]] damage to your patient just before applying the effects of the Treat Wounds. If you do, gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Medicine check to Treat Wounds, and if you roll a success, you get a critical success instead.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.skillfultail.geni: You were born with a tail, and with practice you've learned how to perform simple Interact actions with it, such as opening an unlocked door. Your tail can't perform actions that require fingers or significant manual dexterity, including any action that would require a check to accomplish, and you can't use it to hold items.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.skillfultail.neph: You've always had a tail, but with practice, you've learned to use it for more than signaling your mood. You can perform simple Interact actions with your tail, such as opening an unlocked door. Your tail can't perform actions that require fingers or significant manual dexterity, including any action that would require a check to accomplish, and you can't use it to hold items.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.skillmastery.inve: Increase your proficiency rank in one of your skills from expert to master and in another of your skills from trained to expert. You gain a skill feat associated with one of the skills you chose.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.skillmastery.rogu: Increase your proficiency rank in one of your skills from expert to master and in another of your skills from trained to expert. You gain a skill feat associated with one of the skills you chose; you must meet all its prerequisites.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.skillmastery.spec: You can select this feat up to five times.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.solarrejuvenation: The warmth and light of the sun gives you life. If you rest outdoors for 10 minutes during the day, you regain Hit Points equal to your @Damage[(floor(@actor.level/2)*@actor.abilities.con.mod)[healing]]{Constitution modifier × half your level}. You gain this benefit in addition to any healing from @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.1kGNdIIhuglAjIp9]{Treat Wounds}.

If you rest outdoors for 10 minutes during the day, you regain Hit Points equal to your @Damage[(floor(@actor.level/2)*@actor.abilities.con.mod)[healing]]{Constitution modifier × half your level}. You gain this benefit in addition to any healing from @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.1kGNdIIhuglAjIp9]{Treat Wounds}. Leshies whose plant nourishment does not rely on photosynthesis require a similarly suitable environment. For example, fungus leshies need dark, damp environments and a pile of decaying plant matter.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.yourenext.rogue: After downing a foe, you menace another. Attempt to [[/act demoralize options=youre-next]] a creature within 60 feet, with a +2 circumstance bonus. If you have legendary proficiency in Intimidation, you can use this as a free action with the same trigger.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.yourenext.swashbu: After downing a foe, you promise another that you're coming after them next. Attempt an Intimidation check with a +2 circumstance bonus to [[/act demoralize options=youre-next]] a single creature that you can see and that can see you. If you're legendary in Intimidation, you can use this as a free action with the same trigger.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.feat.yourenext.trigger: You reduce an enemy to 0 Hit Points.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.fetchling.deepfetchlin: Select a type of energy to resist.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.fetchling.lightlesslit: You can Step instead of Striding up to 5 feet.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.fighter.dazingblow.not:
If the Strike hits, the creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|against:class|traits:incapacitation] save against your class DC; this is an incapacitation effect.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2}.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 3}.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.fleshwarp.coatingofsli: On a critical hit, the target takes @Damage[1d4[persistent,acid]] damage.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.fleshwarp.livingweapon: Select a part of your form to use as a weapon.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.fleshwarp.mutateweapon: Select one of your living weapons.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.fortificationlikeeffec: If your armor has a fortification or greater fortification rune, you roll only one flat check.
Each time you're critically hit, attempt a @Check[flat|dc:14|showDC:all]. On a success, it becomes a normal hit.
Each time you're critically hit, attempt a @Check[flat|dc:10|showDC:all]. On a success, it becomes a normal hit.
Each time you're critically hit, attempt a @Check[flat|dc:12|showDC:all]. On a success, it becomes a normal hit.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.frenziedrevelry.note: Whenever someone benefitting from this bonus critically succeeds at a saving throw against an enemy's mental effect, its revelry increases, granting it a +1 status bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls for 1 round. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spell-effects.Item.ldl1aU5ovTQMUHJ2]{Spell Effect: Frenzied Revelry (Critical)}
entries.pf2e.specificrule.gamehunterdedication.s: When you succeed at a Strike against your hunted prey while it's off-guard, it must attempt a @Check[fortitude|against:class] save against your class DC. On a failure, the prey's Speeds are each reduced by 10 feet for 1 round; on a critical failure, the duration of this effect is 1 minute. The prey is then temporarily immune to this effect for 10 minutes. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.eTosCwuCMf09fYYH]{Effect: Game Hunter (Failure)} @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.lfbDqyKUMssoxUTZ]{Effect: Game Hunter (Critical Failure)}
entries.pf2e.specificrule.gamehunterdedication.t: Target is your Hunted Prey (Animal, Beast, or Dragon Only)
entries.pf2e.specificrule.ganzi.irrepressible.sa: When you roll a success on a save against an emotion or fear effect, you get a critical success instead.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.geomancer.terrainattun: You gain a swim Speed equal to your land Speed. If you're on a surface, temporary waves of water follow your movements, allowing you to use your swim Speed—if you have one—as your land Speed. This effect lasts 1 round.
Enemies must attempt a @Check[fortitude] save. They take a –5-foot status penalty to their Speeds for 2 rounds (–10-foot on a critical failure).
Enemies must attempt a @Check[fortitude] save. On a failure, they're @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} until they drink water or another potable liquid.

Branches and vines reach out to get in your foes' way. Enemies must attempt a @Check[reflex] save. On a failure, they become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} for 1 round (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} on a critical failure).
You gain a +1 status bonus to saving throws against the spells and abilities of haunts and undead creatures for 1 round.
You gain resistance to physical damage (except adamantine) equal to the spell level for 1 round.
You gain temporary Hit Points equal to the spell level for 1 round.
You can Fly up to 10 feet. If you're in the air at the end of the turn and don't have a fly Speed, you fall.
Affected foes take @Damage[(max(1,floor(@item.level/2)))[persistent,poison]] damage with a @Check[fortitude|basic] save.
For 1 round, you gain darkvision as well as imprecise tremorsense out to 15 feet.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.ghoul.sickeningbite.cr: When you critically hit a creature with your jaws, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}. This is a disease effect.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.ghoul.sickeningbite.su: You can end your satiation to make the target @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}
entries.pf2e.specificrule.gnome.griminsight.save: When you roll a success on a saving throw against a fear effect, you get a critical success instead, and the source of the fear effect is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to you until the end of your next turn.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.goblin.extrasquishy.no: If you roll a success to Squeeze, you get a critical success instead.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.goblin.mutantgoblin.bi: You gain a bioshock unarmed attack that deals 1d4 electricity damage. Your bioshock attack is in the shock group and has the finesse, nonlethal, and unarmed traits.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.goblin.mutantgoblin.ch: You become trained in Stealth. If you would automatically become trained in Stealth (from your background or class, for example), you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.beyw5bdA5hkQbmaG]{Terrain Stalker} skill feat, except instead of a selected difficult terrain, you can use this feat while adjacent to or within areas of bright artificial light, such as a large neon sign or nightclub.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.goblin.mutantgoblin.ex: You grow a second pair of arms. You can select the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.feats.Item.EPeo604ByeeAx3AL]{All Hands on Deck} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.feats.Item.61txGmMLr22B93cU]{Double Draw} standardized ancestry feats.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.goblin.mutantgoblin.fl: You gain a fly Speed of 20 feet.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.goblin.mutantgoblin.gi: You gain a swim Speed of 25 feet and can breathe underwater for 1 minute at a time.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.goblin.mutantgoblin.li: You can communicate mentally with creatures within 15 feet. You can communicate only with creatures that share a language with you. This doesn't give you any access to their thoughts, and it communicates no more information than normal speech would.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.goblin.mutantgoblin.mu: You gain the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.feats.Item.IEQmMRA1uMPb0rJo]{Ancestral Cantrip} (primal; @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells.Item.thAHF1zxNplLCJPO]{Caustic Blast}) standardized ancestry feat, except the spell doesn't have the manipulate trait as you hock the glob of acid from your mouth.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.goblin.mutantgoblin.su: You gain a climb Speed of 20 feet.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.goblin.recklessabandon: If you roll a failure or critical failure on a saving throw against a harmful effect, you get a success instead.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.goblin.torchgoblin.not: As long as you are suffering persistent fire damage, all your melee attacks against adjacent creatures deal an additional @Damage[1[fire]]{1 fire damage} per weapon damage die.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.goblin.twitchy.initiat: Twitchy — Opponent uses Deception or Diplomacy for initiative
Stymie the Gods — Defending against an extraplanar creature
entries.pf2e.specificrule.goldenbladeofmzali.ele: You have cast elemental form using the Golden Blade of Mzali
entries.pf2e.specificrule.goldenbladeofmzali.lit: Golden Blade of Mzali is lit
entries.pf2e.specificrule.goldenbladeofmzali.per: Unlike normal when taking two types of persistent damage, a creature needs only a single successful flat check to remove both the fire and bleed damage.
When you make an unarmed Strike with your clay fist and hit, your target takes a -2 status penalty to saving throws against curse effects for 1 hour. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.bvk5rwYSoTtz8QGf]{Effect: Accursed Clay Fist}
entries.pf2e.specificrule.greygardener.execution: If you hit, your condemned foe takes an extra @Damage[(8d6[precision])[@item.system.damage.damageType]] damage with a @Check[fortitude|against:class-spell|basic] save. If the condemned foe is reduced to 0 Hit Points by the execution and requires a head to live, they die.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.guardian.bodyguard.tog: Bodyguard — Hostile action against the origin's charge
entries.pf2e.specificrule.guardian.energyinterce: You can use Intercept Attack when an ally would take acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic damage, not only when they would take physical damage.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.guardian.punishingshov: When you successfully Shove a creature, that creature takes @Damage[(@actor.abilities.str.mod + @actor.skills.athletics.rank*(@actor.skills.athletics.rank - 1))[bludgeoning]] damage (or @Damage[(2*(@actor.abilities.str.mod + @actor.skills.athletics.rank*(@actor.skills.athletics.rank - 1)))[bludgeoning]] damage on a critical success).
entries.pf2e.specificrule.guardian.raisehaft.tog: Raise Haft — Weapon already has the parry trait
entries.pf2e.specificrule.guardian.taunt.label: Taunt
entries.pf2e.specificrule.guardian.taunt.note: If you take a hostile action that includes at least one of the guardian's allies but doesn't include them, you also become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until the start of your next turn.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.gunslinger.finalshot.n: Regardless of your roll, after the attack you're @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until the start of your next turn and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.gunslinger.swordandpis: When you make a successful melee Strike against an enemy with your one-handed melee weapon, the next ranged Strike you make against that enemy with a one-handed firearm or one-handed crossbow doesn't trigger reactions that would trigger on a ranged attack but not a melee attack, such as Reactive Strike. This benefit is lost if not used by the end of your next turn.

When you make a successful ranged Strike against an enemy within your reach with your one-handed firearm or one-handed crossbow, that enemy is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} against your next melee attack with a one-handed melee weapon. This benefit is lost if not used by the end of your next turn.
Sword and Pistol — Enemy is within reach
entries.pf2e.specificrule.halfelf.earnedglory.no: If you roll a critical failure when you Make an Impression on an elf, you get a failure instead.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.halfling.guidingluck: You can use Halfling Luck twice per day: once in response to its normal trigger, and once when you fail a Perception check or attack roll instead of the normal trigger.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.halfling.incredibleluc: You can use Halfling Luck once per hour, rather than once per day.
If you have Guiding Luck, you can still use Halfling Luck when you fail a Perception check or attack roll only once per day (though you can use it within the same hour that you used Halfling Luck.)
If you have Shared Luck, you can still use Halfling Luck on an ally instead of yourself only once per day.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.halfling.innocuous.not: If you fail a Deception check to Create a Diversion, humanoid creatures aren't aware that you were trying to trick them unless you get a critical failure on your roll.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.halfling.keeneyes.labe: Seek Hidden or Undetected Creature Within 30 Feet
entries.pf2e.specificrule.halfling.sharedluck.ba: You can use Halfling Luck when an ally within 30 feet fails a skill check or a saving throw to allow the ally to reroll the triggering check instead of you rerolling your own failed check. As usual, your ally must use the new result, even if it's worse than their first roll.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.halfling.sharedluck.gu: You can't use Guiding Luck's effect that applies to attack rolls and Perception checks to use Shared Luck to benefit an ally.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.halfling.surefeet.bala: If you roll a success on an Acrobatics check to Balance, you get a critical success instead. You're not off-guard when you attempt to Balance.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.halfling.surefeet.clim: If you roll a success on an Athletics check to Climb, you get a critical success instead. You're not off-guard when you attempt to Climb.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.harrowchosen.prompt: Does your card match your alignment?
entries.pf2e.specificrule.harrowcourt.bookssuitp: Was the drawn card from Books?
entries.pf2e.specificrule.harrowing.rerolllabel: Rerolling roll using the Harrowing result
entries.pf2e.specificrule.hellknight.revealbeast: Whenever you critically succeed at a check to Recall Knowledge against an animal, beast, fungus, or plant, that creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.heropointdeck.ancestra: Select one of your ancestry boosts, or pick any if it only had free boosts.
Select another one of your ancestry boosts. Decline if it only had free boosts.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.heropointdeck.auraofpr: Select the rank of the spell you just cast.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.heropointdeck.calledfo: This effect lasts until the end of your next turn or until you critically hit the designated foe, whichever comes first.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.heropointdeck.critical: If you still fail this check, you become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 1}.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.heropointdeck.desperat: If the attack fails, you release your weapon, or drop @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} if you were using an unarmed attack or otherwise can't release your weapon.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.hobgoblin.agonizingreb: When you successfully Demoralize a foe, that foe takes @Damage[(ternary(gte(@actor.system.skills.intimidation.rank,4),3,ternary(gte(@actor.system.skills.intimidation.rank,3),2,1))d4)[mental]]{1d4 mental damage} at the start of each of its turns as long as it remains Frightened and continues to engage in combat with you. If you have master proficiency in Intimidation, the damage increases to 2d4, and if you have legendary proficiency, the damage increases to 3d4.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.hobgoblin.cantorianrei: When you succeed at a saving throw against a disease or poison, you reduce the stage by 2 (or 1 for a virulent disease or poison), and when you critically succeed, you reduce the stage by 3 (or 2 for a virulent disease or poison).
entries.pf2e.specificrule.human.saocastrology.pr: Select the circumstance penalty or bonus you obtained when using the Saoc Astrology action.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.human.vikingshieldbear: Select a weapon to become trained in.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.human.witchwarden.save: If you roll a success on a saving throw against a curse or a spell cast by a witch or hag, you get a critical success instead.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.huntprey.firsthitswitc: Hitting Hunted Prey for the First Time This Round
entries.pf2e.specificrule.ifrit.lavasoul.magmasp: Instead of the normal critical specialization effect, the target takes @Damage[1d6[persistent,fire]] damage; you gain a bonus on this persistent damage equal to your item bonus to attack rolls on unarmed attacks.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.impeccableflow.note: If you critically fail any check while under the effects of this spell, the bonus ends and you instead have a -1 status penalty to all checks for the remaining duration.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.impeccableflow.rollopt: Impeccable Flow — You critically failed any check.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.inventor.fullautomatio: You're permanently quickened. You can use the extra action to Stride, Step, or use a form of movement provided by your innovation (such as Fly or Swim).
You're permanently quickened. You can use the extra action to Command your construct innovation (or to provide 1 of the actions if you choose to spend 2 actions to Command your construct).
You're permanently quickened. You can use the extra action to Strike with your innovation.
Select an initial modification to enhance.
Select an initial modification to enhance.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.inventor.overdrive.cri: Whoops! Something explodes. You take @Damage[(ceil(@actor.level / 2))[@actor.flags.system.inventor.explode]|immutable|name:PF2E.SpecificRule.Inventor.Innovation.MalfunctionDamage] damage, and you can't use Overdrive again for [[/r 1d4 #Recharge Overdrive]]{1d4 rounds} as your gizmos reset.
Your gizmos go into a state of incredible efficiency called critical @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.1XlJ9xLzL19GHoOL]{Overdrive}, adding great power to your attacks.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.inventor.overdrive.fai: Your gizmos whine concerningly and begin to smoke. Your Strikes deal 1 additional fire damage. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.1XlJ9xLzL19GHoOL]{Overdrive}
entries.pf2e.specificrule.inventor.overdrive.suc: Your gizmos go into @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.1XlJ9xLzL19GHoOL]{Overdrive}, adding power to your attacks.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.inventor.unstable.fail: You can not use further unstable actions. As the innovation's creator, you can spend 10 minutes retuning your innovation and making adjustments to return it to functionality, at which point you can use unstable actions with that innovation again.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.inventor.unstable.flat: The innovation becomes incapable of being used for further unstable actions, and you also take @Damage[(floor(@actor.level / 2))[@actor.flags.system.inventor.explode]|immutable|name:PF2E.SpecificRule.Inventor.Innovation.MalfunctionDamage] damage. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.olpkQDGDzmYZCvQH]{Effect: Unstable Check Failure}
The innovation becomes incapable of being used for further unstable actions. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.olpkQDGDzmYZCvQH]{Effect: Unstable Check Failure}
entries.pf2e.specificrule.inventor.unstable.note: When you take an unstable action, attempt a @Check[flat|dc:resolve(ternary(lt(@actor.skills.crafting.rank,4),15,13))|options:unstable-check|name:PF2E.SpecificRule.Inventor.Unstable.FlatCheck.Label|showDC:all] immediately after applying its effects.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.investigator.certainst: You deal half of your @Damage[(floor((@actor.level+3)/4)d6[precision])[@item.system.damage.damageType]|options:certain-stratagem]{Strategic Strike damage} to the target
entries.pf2e.specificrule.investigator.detective: You can use Clue In to assist an ally's saving throw against creatures or effects that are related to the question at the heart of an open investigation.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.investigator.justthefa: You are permanently quickened and can use the extra action to Recall Knowledge.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.investigator.sharedstr: The creature you hit is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to a designated ally on the next attack the ally makes against the creature before the start of your next turn.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.jotunborn.iivlarsbound: When you Sustain this spell, increase the distance you teleport to 15 feet.
Jotun's Heart — Increase reach of weapon or fist
entries.pf2e.specificrule.keeppace.bountyhunter: When your hunted prey tries to bolt, you follow. You Stride up to your Speed, following your hunted prey and keeping it in reach throughout its movement until it stops moving or you've moved your full Speed. You can use Keep Pace to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.H6v1VgowHaKHnVlG]{Burrow}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.pprgrYQ1QnIDGZiy]{Climb}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.cS9nfDRGD83bNU1p]{Fly}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.c8TGiZ48ygoSPofx]{Swim} instead of Stride if you have the appropriate movement type.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.keeppace.gamehunter: Once you are upon your prey, they can't escape. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Bcxarzksqt9ezrs6]{Stride} up to your Speed, following the foe and keeping it in reach throughout its movement until it stops moving or you've moved your full Speed. You can use Keep Pace to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.H6v1VgowHaKHnVlG]{Burrow}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.pprgrYQ1QnIDGZiy]{Climb}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.cS9nfDRGD83bNU1p]{Fly}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.c8TGiZ48ygoSPofx]{Swim} instead of Stride if you have the corresponding movement type.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.keeppace.trigger: Your prey is within reach and attempts to move away from you.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.kineticist.extendedkin: You cause an existing element to expand. This works like the generate option, except that you can either create an equal quantity of the element in the same square as its source or in an adjacent square, or cause the element to expand to fill its square (making a flame bigger or turning a twig into a small tree, for example). After you proliferate an element, it reacts to the environment naturally—water you proliferated into thin air would splash back down, for example. This affects only natural forms of the element, not durable, crafted goods.

You change the temperature of an existing element, making it colder or warmer. This can snuff out a flame or turn water to ice or steam. After this change, the element cools or warms naturally unless you Sustain the impulse. An item that gets cold or hot enough (as determined by the GM) can be used as an improvised weapon that deals @Damage[1[cold]] damage or @Damage[1[fire]] damage in addition to the damage the GM determines for the improvised weapon.

Reshape an existing element into a small object of a design you choose. It looks crude and artificial on close examination. The sculpted element can be shaped into a functional short tool (or a long tool if your Base Kinesis can affect 1 Bulk or greater). It's too fragile and artificial to be used as a weapon or have any monetary value.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.kineticist.impulse.cri: Push the target up to 10 feet.
If the target is on the ground, it's knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}; if it isn't on the ground, it descends up to 20 feet (you choose the distance).
The target takes 1d6 persistent fire damage. You gain an item bonus to this persistent damage equal to your item bonus to impulse attack rolls, such as from a gate attenuator.

The target takes 1d6 persistent bleed damage; if the creature has the metal trait or is made of metal, it instead takes @Damage[1d6[persistent,untyped]] damage with no type from rust. You gain an item bonus to this persistent damage equal to your item bonus to impulse attack rolls, such as from a gate attenuator.
The blast deals 2 splash damage per damage die of the blast, with the same damage type.

If the target of the critical hit is adjacent to a surface, roots entangle it. The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} and must spend an Interact action to attempt a @Check[athletics|dc:10] check to pull itself free. The target doesn't become stuck if it is incorporeal, is liquid, or could otherwise escape without effort.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.kineticist.impulse.imp: Before or after the other effects of the impulse, you can either Stride up to half your Speed or Step. If you have a fly Speed, you can Fly up to half your fly Speed instead.

Fragments of stone float around you, granting you a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of your next turn. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.09oP0FBBAhXOS4JW]{Effect: Earth Impulse Junction}

Choose acid, electricity, or piercing. Until the start of your next turn, each time a creature touches you or damages you with an unarmed melee attack or nonreach melee weapon, it takes damage of the chosen type equal to half your level (minimum 1 damage).

After the impulse's other effects, you can move one creature targeted by the impulse or in its area 5 feet in any direction, or 10 feet if it's in a body of water. This can't move the creature into the air. You can choose only a creature that's willing to be moved, that failed its save against the impulse, or that you succeeded at an impulse attack roll against.
You gain temporary Hit Points equal to your level that last until the start of your next turn. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.rCsmv66TzQhte4Gp]{Effect: Wood Impulse Junction}
entries.pf2e.specificrule.kineticist.kineticgate: Select a radius for your kinetic aura.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.kineticist.overflow.de: When you use an impulse that has the overflow trait, your kinetic aura deactivates until you revitalize it (typically with @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.g8QrV39TmZfkbXgE]{Channel Elements}). You can use only one overflow impulse per round, even if you reactivate your kinetic gate.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.kobold.draconicparagon: Creatures that critically fail their save against your Kobold Breath take @Damage[3d4[persistent,damage]].
entries.pf2e.specificrule.kobold.dragonbloodpara: Creatures that critically fail their save against your Kobold Breath take @Damage[3d4[@actor.flags.system.draconicExemplar.damageType,persistent]].
entries.pf2e.specificrule.kobold.dragonsbreath.d: You channel your draconic exemplar's power into a gout of energy that manifests as a @Template[line|distance:60] or a @Template[cone|distance:30], dealing @Damage[ceil(@actor.level/2)d8[@actor.flags.system.draconicExemplar.damageType]|options:area-damage] damage. Each creature in the area must attempt a basic saving throw against the higher of your class DC or spell DC. You can't use this ability again for 1 hour.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.kobold.dragonspresence: When you roll a success on a saving throw against a fear effect, you get a critical success instead. When you roll a failure against a fear effect, you get a critical failure instead.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.lashunta.ascendantpsyc: Increase the range of your limited telepathy to 120 feet.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.lepidstadtsurgeon.dedi: When you successfully Administer First Aid to stabilize a dying creature that doesn't yet have the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Yl48xTdMh3aeQYL2]{Wounded} condition, it regains @Damage[(2d8 + 10*max(0,(@actor.system.skills.medicine.rank - 2)))[healing]] Hit Points.

When you successfully Administer First Aid to stop bleeding, the target rolls the flat check (with lowered DC for an assisted recovery) twice and takes the better result; this is a fortune effect. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.45nzYsMBTP6PIxDN]{Effect: Lepidstadt Surgeon Dedication (Stop Bleeding)}
entries.pf2e.specificrule.leshy.seedpod.note: On a critical hit, a seedpod bursts, issuing forth a tangle of vegetation that imposes a -10-foot circumstance penalty on the target's Speed for 1 round. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.wQDHpOKY3GZqvS2v]{Effect: Seedpod}
entries.pf2e.specificrule.leshy.thornedseedpod.n: When you critically succeed at a seedpod Strike, vines twist and stab your foe, dealing @Damage[1d4[persistent,piercing]] damage.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.lich.handofthelich.not: The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} until the end of your next turn unless it succeeds at a @Check[fortitude|against:spell] save against your spell DC.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.lizardfolk.parthenogen: Each of your successful saving throws against a disease reduces its stage by 2, or by 1 for a virulent disease. Each critical success against an ongoing disease reduces its stage by 3, or by 2 for a virulent disease.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.magicalshorthand.note: If you fail, you can try again after a week or after you gain a level, whichever is first.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.magus.spellstrike.rech: You recharge your Spellstrike when you cast a conflux spell that takes at least 1 action to cast.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.magus.unsheathingthesw: Countless copies of your sword, made of light, fall around you, dealing @Damage[(2*{item|flags.pf2e.rulesSelections.unsheathingTheSwordLight})[@item.system.damage.damageType]|options:area-damage,damage:material:cold-iron,damage:material:silver] damage to each creature within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] of the target, excluding you and the target of the Spellstrike.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.mantleoftheunwaveringh: Mantle of the Unwavering Heart — First ability
Mantle of the Unwavering Heart — Second ability
entries.pf2e.specificrule.markfordeath.deceivema: To use Deception to Feint your Mark
entries.pf2e.specificrule.marshal.dreadmarshalst: When you critically hit an enemy with a Strike, that enemy is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.martialartist.grievous: This Strike ignores an amount of resistance to physical damage (or to a specific physical damage type) equal to your level.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.martialproficiency.bou: Bounty Hunter Tools of the Trade
entries.pf2e.specificrule.mechanicaltorch.label: Mechanical Torch is lit
entries.pf2e.specificrule.merfolk.reefmerfolk.cr: You reduce the stage of the poison by 3.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.merfolk.reefmerfolk.su: You reduce the stage of the poison by 2.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.merfolk.reefmerfolk.vi: You reduce the stage of the virulent poison by 2.
You reduce the stage of the virulent poison by 1.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.minotaur.siphontorment: The target creature gains temporary Hit Points equal to your level
entries.pf2e.specificrule.monk.dragonroar.label: First attack vs. frightened creature
entries.pf2e.specificrule.monk.fivegodsram.first: You immediately push the target back 10 feet.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.monk.stunningfist.note: The target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|against:monk|name:Monk|traits:incapacitation|overrideTraits:true] save against your class DC or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 3} on a critical failure).
entries.pf2e.specificrule.monk.wolfdrag.note: If the attack succeeds, you knock the target @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.mountainstance.bracers: Mountain Stance w/ Bracers of Armor
entries.pf2e.specificrule.mummy.accursedtouch.no: While the creature is taking this persistent damage, it's also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1}.
While the creature is taking this persistent damage, it's also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1}.
While the creature is taking this persistent damage, it's also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1}.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.musicalshift.flat.note: Whenever the enemy critically fails at a Strike, saving throw, or skill check, they fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} in addition to other effects from the critical failure.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.musicalshift.sharp.not: Whenever an enemy takes piercing or slashing damage, they also take @Damage[2d6[bleed]] damage.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.mythic.pointspentpromp: Was a Mythic Point spent on this ability?
entries.pf2e.specificrule.nagaji.venomspit.criti: The target takes @Damage[(@weapon.system.damage.dice)[persistent,poison]]{persistent poison damage} equal to the number of weapon damage dice
entries.pf2e.specificrule.nagaji.venomspit.hoode: The target is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} until the start of your next turn.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.nidalesehorselord.defy: You increase the amount of damage you take by @Damage[4d6[untyped]], and deal @Damage[4d6[spirit]] damage to the triggering fiend.
You increase the amount of damage you take by @Damage[5d6[untyped]], and deal @Damage[5d6[spirit]] damage to the triggering fiend.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.notes.requirementsnotm: The creature does not currently meet the requirements to use this ability.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.oracle.conduitofvoidan: You cast a 3-action harm spell, expending the slot as normal. If the spell restores Hit Points to one or more creatures, then one creature healed by this spell regains @Damage[(@actor.conditions.cursebound.value)d8[healing,void]|shortLabel:true] additional Hit Points; if the spell damages one or more creatures, then one creature harmed by this spell takes @Damage[(@actor.conditions.cursebound.value)d8[void]|shortLabel:true] additional damage.

You cast a 3-action heal spell, expending the slot as normal. If the spell restores Hit Points to one or more creatures, then one creature healed by this spell regains @Damage[(@actor.conditions.cursebound.value)d8[healing,vitality]|shortLabel:true] additional Hit Points; if the spell damages one or more creatures, then one creature harmed by this spell takes @Damage[(@actor.conditions.cursebound.value)d8[vitality]|shortLabel:true] additional damage.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.oracle.cursebound.incr: When you use a cursebound ability, you become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.zXZjC8HLaRoLR17U]{Cursebound 1}, or if you already were cursebound, you increase the value of your cursebound condition by 1.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.oracle.cursebound.maxi: You can't use a cursebound ability if you are already at your maximum @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.zXZjC8HLaRoLR17U]{Cursebound} value.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.oracle.curseseverity.m: When you cast a revelation spell while affected by your major curse, your curse intensifies to an extreme effect. When affected by your extreme curse, you become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 2} (or increase your doomed condition by 2 if you were already doomed). Once every 10 minutes, when you fail an attack roll, skill or Perception check, or saving throw, you can reroll it and use the second result. The reroll has the fortune trait and doesn't require you to spend an action, meaning you can use the reroll even if you can't act.
If you cast a revelation spell while your minor curse is in effect, you progress to the moderate curse effect immediately after you finish Casting the Spell.
You become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} in addition to the effects of the minor curse.
Immediately after completing the casting of a revelation spell while you are affected by your moderate curse, your curse progresses to its major effect.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.oracle.curseseverity.n: Your curse progresses to its minor stage the first time you finish casting a revelation spell after your daily preparations.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.oracle.curseseverity.o: Immediately after casting a revelation spell, you are overwhelmed. While overwhelmed, you can't Cast or Sustain any revelation spells
entries.pf2e.specificrule.oracle.meddlingfutures: If you attempt to use a different action, you must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:6] or the action is lost.
When you cast a spell of 5th rank or lower that has no duration, instead of spending a spell slot, you can advance the effects of your oracular curse in the same manner as when you cast a revelation spell. A spell cast in this way gains the cursebound trait.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.oracle.revelinretribut: Lashing out at a defenseless enemy invigorates you with the thrill of combat, granting you temporary Hit Points whenever you successfully hit with a Reactive Strike. The temporary Hit Points last for the spell's duration. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spell-effects.Item.rnkQKEwUxrGruE1e]{Spell Effect: Revel in Retribution (Temp HP)}
entries.pf2e.specificrule.orc.orcwarmask.prompt: Choose the source of your warmask's power.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.packattack.label: Target is Within Range of At Least Two Allies
entries.pf2e.specificrule.parry.prompt: Does your weapon have the parry trait?
entries.pf2e.specificrule.pfs.narsensweb.rollopt: Hiding behind a mask or disguise
entries.pf2e.specificrule.poppet.washout.note: Each time you succeed at a Fortitude save against an ongoing poison, you reduce its stage by 2, or by 1 against a virulent poison. Each critical success you achieve against an ongoing poison reduces its stage by 3, or by 2 against a virulent poison.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.powderpunchstance.note: The target is pushed back an additional 5 feet.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.prompt.advancedmulticl: Select a class feat up to half your character level.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.prompt.classdcabilitys: Select the class DC's key attribute.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.prompt.weapondamagedic: Select a number of weapon damage dice.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.prompt.weaponorunarmed: Select a weapon or unarmed strike.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.astralrain: The area changes to a @Template[burst|distance:5], and you can Sustain the Spell up to 1 minute. The first time each round that you Sustain the Spell, you can move the burst up to 20 feet.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.contagious: You can choose to spread the thought from the second target to a third target, in the same manner as spreading it from the first target to the second. A pleasant thought grants twice as many temporary Hit Points (10, plus an additional 2 for each rank heightened).
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.dancingbla: The weapon's attacks increase in strength, and your control is fine enough for advanced technique. The damage dice for the weapon's Strike change from d6s to d10s. When you Cast or Sustain the spell, you can choose from the following options in addition to the standard ones.

Rather than attacking, the weapon grants a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against melee attacks to the creature it's following. The bonus lasts until the start of your next turn or until the weapon Changes Partners, whichever comes first.
(attack) The weapon attempts to Push its target, using your spell attack modifier instead of an Athletics check to determine the results of the Push.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.daze.base: Your spell cracks the target's mental defenses, leaving it susceptible to further psychic attack. The spell's damage changes to 1d10. If the target fails its Will save, until the end of its next turn, it gains weakness 1 to mental damage and takes a –1 status penalty to Will saves. On a critical failure, the weakness is 3 (in addition to the target being @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}). The weakness applies before daze deals damage.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.daze.heigh: The spell's damage increases by 2d10, and the weakness on a failure or critical failure increases by 1.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.detectmagi: Your pulse of detection magic helps you gain a sense of nearby magic's strengths or weaknesses. When you detect the presence of magic on an enemy or hazard, you gain a +1 status bonus to saves against magic originating from that enemy or hazard for 3 rounds. If you find magic from multiple sources with a single casting of _detect magic_, you get the bonus against all of them.
The status bonus to saves against magic increases to +2.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.distortion: You can lock the lens in place, allowing it to affect creatures more strongly. The duration of the spell becomes 1 minute, but you cannot Sustain the spell to reposition the lens. Once during a Medium or smaller ally's move action, the ally can move into and out of the lens's area without those spaces counting against the total distance moved. Conversely, the lens is difficult terrain for your enemies.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.entropicwh: You gain two motes instead of one when you Cast the Spell and each time the wheel gains another mote.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.figment: When you amp the spell, you can create a particularly distracting illusion as part of its normal effects. Choose an unoccupied square within the spell's range. The illusion in that square provides flanking for a single melee attack made before the beginning of your next turn. If you Sustain the spell, the details of the illusion change and shift to keep your enemies unsettled; the flanking illusion's duration extends until the beginning of your next turn and you can move it to any unoccupied square in the spell's range. The flanking illusion can't provide its benefit against any creature who has disbelieved the _figment_.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.forbiddent: Your telepathic push is particularly shocking to those who resist it. If the target fails its save, it's also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.foreseethe: The spell has the same result on a failure as on a success (but still no effect on a critical failure). Each time you Sustain the Spell, you can choose an additional ally to target, granting them the same benefit as the initial target ally.
You drain thermal energy at a distance, using what you plunder to replenish yourself. The orb deals 3d4 cold damage. You gain temporary Hit Points equal to half the damage the target takes (after applying resistances and the like). You lose any remaining temporary Hit Points after 1 minute.
The initial damage increases by 2d4 instead of 1d4. The weakness on a critical failure increases by 1.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.ghostlyshi: Your insubstantiality is complete enough that you can pass through other creatures, causing painful interference effects when you do so. Once each round when you Stride, Burrow, Swim, or Fly, you can pass through one creature's space during that action without needing to attempt a check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.21WIfSu7Xd7uKqV8]{Tumble Through}, though you can't end your turn in its space. When you pass through a creature's space in this way, you deal 2d6 force damage to that creature, with a @Check[fortitude|basic] save.
Increase the damage dealt by phasing through a creature by 1d6.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.glimpsewea: The additional precision damage increases by 1d4+1.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.guidance.b: You can project a flash of insight to your ally in the nick of time to save them from failure. Amped _guidance_ doesn't cause a creature to become temporarily immune to _guidance_, and a creature can be targeted by amped _guidance_ even if it is temporarily immune to _guidance_.

You can cast an amped _guidance_ spell as a reaction triggered when your ally fails or critically fails an attack roll, Perception check, saving throw, or skill check, and the bonus from _guidance_ would change the failure to a success or the critical failure to a normal failure. The bonus from _guidance_ applies retroactively to their check.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.guidance.h: The bonus from the spell increases to +2.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.hologramca: Force walls make the cage's barrier physical as well as visual. Each 10-foot-by-10-foot section of the cage's wall has AC 10, Hardness equal to double the spell's rank, and HP equal to quadruple the spell's rank. The spell is disrupted if any of its walls would pass through a creature when you cast it.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.homingbeac: Choose one creature hit by the spell (if the spell has a spell attack roll) or that fails its save against the spell (if the spell requires a save). A psychic beacon emits from the creature's location, making it easier to notice the creature as the mind and attention of onlookers is subtly drawn to it. For 1 minute, if the target would be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible}, it's instead merely @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}, and if it would be concealed, it's no longer concealed.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.ignition.b: You project pure heat that causes a target to combust. The initial damage changes to 1d10 fire damage plus 1 fire splash damage. When using amped _ignition_ as a melee attack, increase the damage dice of the initial damage from d10s to d12s. You are not harmed by splash damage from amped _ignition_.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.ignition.h: Instead of using _ignition_'s normal heightened entry, the initial damage increases by 1d10 (1d12 for melee) and the splash damage increases by 1. The persistent fire damage on a critical hit increases by 1d4.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.imaginaryw: You form multiple force weapons to lash out at multiple foes. You can target two creatures, making an attack against each. These attacks each increase your multiple attack penalty, but you don't increase your multiple attack penalty until after you make all the spell attack rolls.
The damage increases by 2d6 instead of 1d6.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.inertialba: You or one target of the spell gains resistance to physical damage equal to 2 + the spell's rank until the start of your next turn.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.mentalbalm: You or one ally within 30 feet gains a +2 status bonus to Will saves against emotion effects for 1 minute. You can also have the amped psi cantrip attempt to counteract one effect imposing the frightened condition on yourself or the chosen ally, or an effect imposing the stupefied condition that has a duration of 1 hour or less. If you successfully counteract the effect, you remove only the frightened and stupefied conditions, not any other part of the effect.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.message.ba: Your telepathic message allows you to coordinate your actions while you pass on information. The target of the message can immediately spend its reaction to Step or Stride.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.message.he: The target of the message can choose to [[/act shove]], Strike, or [[/act trip]] with its reaction instead.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.mindslight: You transfer some of the magical luminescence from the mind's light circlet to one of the creatures. Choose a creature targeted by the spell or in its area. Until the start of your next turn, that creature sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius (and dim light to the next 20 feet) and can't be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}. If the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible}, it's concealed while alight, rather than being @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VRSef5y1LmL2Hkjf]{Undetected}.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.omnidirect: Your scans are so minute that you can project your foe's likely strengths and weaknesses. You learn which of the target enemy's saving throws is highest and which is lowest, and you and all allies within 30 feet gain a +1 circumstance bonus to attacks and damage against the creature.

If you take the Aid reaction you prepared for and succeed at your roll, these bonuses increase to +2 on the attack you're Aiding. On a critical success, they increase to +3, or to +4 if you have legendary proficiency in spell attack modifier.
Your bolt leaves phase completely, becoming invisible and intangible until it's already embedded in the target—giving the impression it simply teleported itself into place. The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} against the attack. Additionally, the bolt ignores an amount of Hardness or resistance to piercing damage equal to half the spell's rank. On a critical success, the target can't be affected by teleportation effects until the start of your next turn.
The bolt's damage increases by 2d4 instead of 1d4.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.redistribu: You can affect the flow of energy on a much grander scale. Instead of two 5-foot squares, you create two @Template[burst|distance:10]{10-foot bursts}. They still must be adjacent to one another and can overlap. A creature in both areas can still be affected by only one of your choice. The spell's damage increases to 6d6. The conditions last for 1 minute.
Increase the damage by 2d6 instead of 1d4.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.removepres: Choose one target of the spell or one creature in its area. If that enemy fails its save or the spell hits it, you become undetected by that creature, disappearing from its senses for 1 minute or until you use a hostile action. This is similar to being @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible}, but effects like see the unseen don't reveal you—you're affecting the target's mind, not its vision. _Truesight_ can still see through this illusion if the counteract check succeeds.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.shattermin: You increase the area of the spell to a @Template[cone|distance:30] or @Template[cone|distance:60], and the damage dice for the spell change to d10s. Creatures that fail are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} until the start of your next turn (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} on a critical failure).
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.shatterspa: You or one target of the spell are surrounded by a spiral of torn space. This is an aura, and you choose the radius of the emanation, up to 20 feet. A creature that starts its turn in the aura or enters the aura during its turn takes @Damage[(@item.level)d6[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage for every rank of the amped psi cantrip, with a @Check[reflex|basic|options:area-effect] save against your spell DC. If the creature critically fails, the torn space throws it off balance and it falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. The aura lasts until the beginning of your next turn. You can choose whether the aura affects the creature emitting it.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.shield.bas: You create a layered barrier of three shields to improve your defenses. The duration increases to sustained up to 1 minute. The shield still lowers at the start of each of your turns, but you can raise it again when you Sustain the Spell.

When you Shield Block with this spell, one of the three layers breaks and you cease to gain the bonus to AC, but the spell doesn't end. You can keep using the spell until the final layer breaks; once it does, the spell ends and you can't cast shield or amped shield again for 10 minutes. You can choose to break additional layers when you Shield Block with this spell to improve your defenses. If you break two layers instead of one, increase the Hardness to 1-1/2 × the normal amount (7 at 1st rank, 15 at 3rd rank, 22 at 5th rank, 30 at 7th rank, and 37 at 9th rank). If you break all three layers, increase the Hardness to double the normal amount.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.shield.rol: Shield (Amped) — Break layers to Shield Block
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.targetofps: Choose one target of the spell. Psychic phenomena turn themselves on the target—typically, objects fling themselves at the creature. At the beginning of each of the target's turns, it takes @Damage[(@item.level)d4[bludgeoning]] damage, with a @Check[reflex|against:occult|basic] save. The target remains marked by psychic ire for 3 rounds. The effect persists even if you fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} or leave the area.
entries.pf2e.specificrule.psychic.amp.telekineti: You create a multitude of telekinetic hands that grip onto a creature and move it about. Target a creature of Medium size or smaller with the amped spell instead of an object. You attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.7blmbDrQFNfdT731]{Shove} the target in a direction of your choice, rolling a spell attack roll against its Fortitude DC instead of an Athletics check. The creature takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds until the spell ends. Starting the round after you Cast the Spell, the first time each round you Sustain the Spell, you can attempt to Shove the creature again.

You can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Dt6B1slsBy8ipJu9]{Disarm} the creature instead of attempting to Shove it. If you knock an item out of the creature's grasp in this way, the _telekinetic hand_ immediately grabs it. Any effect of the _telekinetic hand_ on the creature ends, and the spell now carries the item, just like you had picked it up with an unamped _telekinetic hand_.

You fling objects with even more force, driving your opponents backwards in a hail of objects. On a success, you push the target 5 feet away from you, and on a critical success, you push the target 10 feet away from you in addition to dealing double damage.
The damage increases by 2d6 instead of by 1d6.
